key_insight: Lattice reconstruction in TaCo$_2$Te$_2$ reshapes the electronic symmetry through orbital hybridization, which suppresses the magnetic instability and stabilizes a nonmagnetic ground state.
engineering_relevance: The work shows that tuning structural reconstruction in low-dimensional materials can be a viable route to control magnetism and correlated electronic phases.
method: STM/STS to map bias-dependent electronic contrast and local density of states
nc-AFM to resolve the reconstructed Te surface lattice
ARPES plus orbital-projected DFT to connect Fermi-surface anisotropy, electronic reconstruction, and magnetic suppression
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent STM/STS, nc-AFM, ARPES, and DFT evidence that the reconstructed structure produces anisotropic low-energy states and a nonmagnetic ground state.
watch_item: Bias-dependent STM contrast reflects an energy-integrated LDOS and may not directly correspond to atomic positions, so interpretation requires combining microscopy with spectroscopy and theory.
paper_title: Lattice Reconstruction and Orbital Hybridization Suppress Magnetism in TaCo$_2$Te$_2$
